title: new "image" mount type
body:
Add a new "image" mount type to `--mount`.  The source of the mount is 
the name or ID of an image.  The destination is the path inside the    
container.  Image mounts further support an optional `rw,readwrite`    
parameter which if set to "true" will yield the mount writable inside  
the container.  Note that no changes are propagated to the image mount 
on the host (which in any case is read only).                          
                                                                       
Mounts are overlay mounts.  To support read-only overlay mounts, vendor
a non-release version of Buildah.                                      

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I think you're missing most of the conflict resolution logic for volumes
with the same destination - check container_validate.go in Libpod.

> I think you're missing most of the conflict resolution logic for volumes with the same destination - check container_validate.go in Libpod.

Thanks for checking! I stopped at this point as I want to check with @rhatdan if the mounts should be RO or if we make use of overlay mounts. The latter may allow for more use cases.

@mheon @rhatdan @mrunalp please have a look.

I'll update the man pages once we're good to go. Image volumes are now always overlay mounts so that they're writeable inside the container. There's currently a rootless overlay-mount bug during cleanup, so you may ignore errors on container removal.
@edsantiago feel free to check the system tests if there's a potential blocker from your side

Please ensure these new mounts are present in the output of `podman inspect`

@vrothberg have you looked into the failing tests? The common factor is root system tests, on all OS, local and remote, and the failing test is `touch /image-mount/read-only` (it is succeeding, but the test expects it to fail)

> @vrothberg have you looked into the failing tests? The common factor is root system tests, on all OS, local and remote, and the failing test is `touch /image-mount/read-only` (it is succeeding, but the test expects it to fail)

Thanks for checking! I'll wrap things up in a moment. I wanted to use overlay mounts but those are RW by default, so it had to update the overlay package in Buildah to support RO mounts.

Now, the image mounts are RO (again) and RW by choice.
Note: this PR vendors a non-release Buildah. I think that's okay as we need to get short-names support in before cutting Podman v2.2.
Thanks for the reviews, @mheon. Wrapped it up and remote sys tests should be green in a bit.
